description: Refactor TaskPanel.jsx (sidebar) to separate viewing from editing: single click selects a task and shows a read-only notes preview beneath it, double click or Edit button opens edit mode. Increase sidebar width, reduce spacing for better information density.
steps: In src/TaskPanel.jsx, change the top-level container width from 'w-80' (320px) to 'w-96' (384px) to give more horizontal room for task information.

In the task display mode section (the non-editing branch of the task row rendering), ensure single click (handleTaskClick) only sets selectedTaskId without opening any edit form. The existing code already does this correctly.

When a task is selected (selectedTaskId === task.id) and NOT in edit mode, show a compact read-only notes preview directly beneath the task row. This should display only task.notes content in a small read-only div (e.g., a pale background div with the notes text). Do NOT show editable fields, dropdowns, buttons, or forms in this preview.
Ensure double-click (handleTaskDoubleClick) opens the full edit form as it currently does (calls startEditTask).
Ensure the Edit button (Edit3 icon in the hover action buttons) also opens edit mode as it currently does.
Reduce vertical padding on task rows: change 'py-1' to 'py-0.5' or similar to increase task density.
Reduce spacing in the Row 2 (Group, Priority, Due Date) meta line: make it more compact.

The notes preview should be minimal: only show if notes exist, use 'text-[11px] text-slate-500 px-3 py-1 bg-slate-50/50' or similar light styling, show max 2-3 lines with overflow-hidden and a subtle truncation.
acceptance_criteria: Single clicking a task row highlights it and shows read-only notes beneath it, without opening any edit form.
Double clicking a task row opens the full edit form with all fields.
Clicking the Edit (pencil) button on hover also opens the full edit form.
The sidebar width is increased from w-80 to w-96.
Task rows have reduced vertical spacing so more tasks are visible.
Notes preview is read-only - no inputs, dropdowns, or buttons.
All existing functionality (task groups, colors, location system, ordering, persistence, status system) remains intact.
npm run build succeeds without errors.
verification: Run `npm run build` and confirm it completes successfully without errors.
